description:
Maker 3D CAD — the middle ground between Tinkercad and FreeCAD.

Materializr is open-source 3D CAD for makers — the middle ground between
dead-simple tools like Tinkercad and full professional suites like FreeCAD.
Sketch on a plane, pull it into a solid (extrude, revolve, loft, sweep),
fillet and chamfer, run booleans, cut threads, and edit any step of the
history later. Sketches export to SVG at 1:1 mm for laser cutters and 2.5D
CNC; it imports STEP and IGES and exports STEP, STL, IGES, glTF and SVG. Real
B-rep solids on the OpenCASCADE kernel.
Materializr 1.4.0 — the biggest release yet: smarter edits, an iPad-ready codebase, and onboarding that meets you where you are.
Highlights
Edits that survive. Materializr now tracks which faces and edges your features were built on, not just where they were. Fillets and chamfers placed on boolean seam edges follow the model through upstream edits; push/pull face targets survive sketch changes; and every operation type — including plane/axis moves, lofts, sweeps, mirrors, splits, copies and aligns — reloads from a saved project fully editable. Open an old project, change step 1, and the whole history replays.
Threads, fast. Thread creation was rebuilt on a swept-profile engine: what took minutes (and a screaming fan) on a 14×70 mm rod now takes about a fifth of a second. Long rods, tapers and holes all keep their existing quality; recomputes happen off the UI thread so cascading edits never freeze the app.
Choose your workspace. First launch now opens with a layout picker — Classic, Modern, or im-touch — with a live preview: tap an option and the whole app switches behind the dialog. The Getting Started tour then teaches the app in the layout you picked, pointing at the actual buttons on your screen.
Chromebook & Intel tablet support. The Play Store build now ships x86_64 alongside arm64.
iPad. The codebase now builds for iOS/iPadOS (App Store review in progress), including a tip-jar Supporter option. The same Supporter state is honored across platforms.
Sketching
- Symmetry inference now suggests the mirror and point-reflection of your previous point through the last placed point — the suggestions you actually want mid-drawing — instead of degenerate bounding-box mirrors.
- Hover-to-charge alignment guides no longer latch onto references you never hovered (drive-by charging at coarse grids is gone, and out-of-plane geometry no longer projects invisible snap magnets into your sketch).
- Undo while drawing a spline removes one point at a time, like lines.
- Face, edge and vertex selections now show full measurement readouts (area, radius, length, sweep, centre…), with totals across multi-selections.
- The Measure tool lives in the View menu in every layout.
SVG round trip
- Exported sketches now write connected loops as single closed paths, true <circle> elements and real arc commands — and re-import recovers native circles, splines and shared-corner line loops instead of duplicated fragments that never closed.
- Hairline strokes (laser/CAM cut-line convention) import as the curve itself rather than a ribbon outline.
Modern layout
- Drag the tool rail wider and it reflows into a two-column grid — half the scrolling on tablets.
- Copy/Mirror/Duplicate group under Transform; Linear/Circular under Pattern; inference level moved to the top bar.
- Panel fold chevrons now appear reliably on cold start; Properties gets more room and History scrolls.
Fixes & platform
- macOS: app no longer aborts on launch on newer macOS (SDL2 rebuild) — #12.
- Frozen-step warnings on reload are gone for new saves (every op serializes now), and the warning text is honest about the cause.
- Welcome screen sequencing: first launch is the tour's moment; the welcome begins on the next launch.
- Licensing: GPLv3 with section-7 additional permissions (app-store distribution, platform SDKs) — see LICENSE-EXCEPTIONS.md. Privacy policy added.
Downloads
Desktop assets (Linux AppImage x86_64/aarch64, Windows installer + portable, macOS Apple Silicon .dmg) are attached below. Android APKs: arm64-v8a and, new, x86_64.
